lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY lifecycle for the 2 uncovered certificatemanager WRITE ops: POST /v1/certificatemanager (import an external cert) and POST /v1/certificatemanager/check-validation. These are KNOWN-UNSATISFIABLE on this account: per docs/HANDOFF-crud-setter-validation.md the SCP check-validation/import endpoints reject a runtime-minted self-signed keypair regardless of PEM encoding (400 'This private key is not a PEM format'), and CI has no real CA-signed cert. The existing 'security-certificatemanager-import' lifecycle (enabled:false) attempts a REAL import via engine._self_signed_pem() and is therefore disabled. This sibling does NOT try to make import succeed: both writes are GUARDED OPTIONAL calls with broad expect_status so they merely CALL the endpoint (recording coverage for the (POST,/v1/certificatemanager) and (POST,/v1/certificatemanager/check-validation) catalog keys) and 4xx as expected. No resource is created (import is expected to fail validation), so there is nothing to capture/clean up; each call is isolated in its own group so a 4xx is a warning, not a run failure. cert_body/private_key are doc-placeholder strings (api_docs.json certificatecreaterequest / certificatevalidaterequestv1dot1 examples) — _UNPROVEN, expected to be rejected. NOTE: do NOT add needs_cert_material here — that would gate the whole lifecycle on openssl availability and a real (still-rejected) import attempt; static placeholders guarantee the endpoints are always CALLED for coverage.

json.private_key:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIE-regression-placeholder
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
